schemaEvidence.conclusion: No field distinguishes when T chip data became available vs just the trade date. Cannot confirm T+0 chip availability by schema alone.

cronEvidence.effectiveLag: T-1 (prior trading day) via scheduled cron
cronEvidence.manualRetriggerWindow: T+0 chip data possible if cron manually retriggered after 17:30 TWN — not the default production path

gateEvidence.conclusion: PIT gate is present and correctly applied. Gate does not prevent T+0 leakage if chip data is pre-loaded before cron normalization, but cron evidence shows effective lag = T-1.

c_f05AssumptionAudit.assumptionText: T+0 institutional chip data published ~6pm on T. Post-close scoring assumption: scoring runs after 6pm (T) or uses prior day data. Pre-market intraday use not supported.
c_f05AssumptionAudit.cronPath: Uses prior day data (T-1) — the 'prior day data' branch is the actual production path
c_f05AssumptionAudit.manualPath: T+0 achievable if manual trigger after ~17:30 TWN
c_f05AssumptionAudit.assumptionConsistency: CONSISTENT — assumption correctly documents both branches; cron produces T-1 branch in practice
c_f05AssumptionAudit.verdict: C-F05 assumption is not violated — it explicitly covers the T-1 case. However, no production log evidence confirms T+0 was ever achieved via cron.

upgradePath: 1. Add availableAt DateTime field to prisma.InstitutionalChip schema.
2. Set availableAt on upsert in syncInstitutionalChip() to record fetch time.
3. Adjust cron to fire AFTER T86 availability (~18:00 TWN = 10:00 UTC).
4. Verify T+0 data availability via production sync logs.
5. After steps 1-4, re-audit to advance classification to CHIP_LAG_CONFIRMED.
disclaimer: Structural audit-only. Does not constitute investment advice. No financial claims, win-rate, edge, or outperformance claims. Results must not be used as buy/sell/hold signals.
